entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: The fifth most important cereal crop grown for food, feed, fodder, and bioenergy purposes.

entities.name: MARGINAL ENVIRONMENTS
entities.description: Environments where sorghum is a staple for over 500 million resource-poor people.

entities.name: ICRISAT
entities.description: International Crops Research Institute for the Semi-Arid Tropics, an organization in India conserving sorghum germplasm.

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where ICRISAT is located.

entities.name: USDA-ARS
entities.description: The Plant Genetic Resources Conservation Unit, Southern Regional Plant Introduction Station, University of Georgia, USDA-ARS.

entities.name: GERMPLASM ACCESSIONS
entities.description: Genetic resources conserved in genebanks, totaling over 236,000 for sorghum.

entities.name: CORE AND MINI CORE COLLECTIONS
entities.description: Representative subsets of germplasm diversity used for identifying trait sources.

entities.name: GENOTYPING-BASED REFERENCE SET
entities.description: A set established in sorghum to provide access to large genetic diversity.

entities.name: BIOTIC AND ABIOTIC STRESS RESISTANCE
entities.description: Resistance to various biological and environmental stresses identified in sorghum collections.

entities.name: AGRONOMIC AND GRAIN NUTRITIONAL TRAITS
entities.description: Important characteristics related to crop performance and nutritional value.

entities.name: RESEQUENCING
entities.description: The process of sequencing diverse germplasm resources to relate sequence variations with phenotypic traits.

entities.name: WILD AND WEEDY RELATIVES
entities.description: Relatives of sorghum used in resequencing studies to expand genetic resources.

entities.name: SEQUENCE VARIATIONS
entities.description: Variations in DNA sequences that can be related to phenotypic traits of interest.

entities.name: PHENOTYPIC TRAITS
entities.description: Observable characteristics of the plant used for improvement and selection.

entities.name: SORGHUM IMPROVEMENT
entities.description: The overall goal of utilizing genetic resources and mapping to enhance the crop.

entities.name: GENOMEWIDE ASSOCIATION MAPPING
entities.description: Studies used to identify genomic regions associated with agronomic traits and stress resistance.

entities.name: GENOMIC REGIONS
entities.description: Specific areas of the genome associated with important traits and stress resistance.

entities.name: HIGH-THROUGHPUT PHENOTYPING
entities.description: Platforms and technologies required for precise phenotyping to attain greater genetic gains.
